biggest_disaster.evidence: 8.2 complexity score when CLAUDE.md mandates <5. This isn't 'hybrid intelligence', it's hybrid stupidity.
biggest_disaster.roast: 536 lines to do what 50 could accomplish. You've created the Microsoft Word of thinking commands.

most_delusional.evidence: 44 thinking blocks in one command. That's not intelligent, that's paranoid.
most_delusional.roast: You've confused 'enhanced' with 'enhanced interrogation'. This command thinks more than a philosophy PhD student.

participation_trophy.evidence: Only command under complexity score 5, but still violates CLAUDE.md simplicity rules
participation_trophy.roast: Congratulations! You're the tallest dwarf in the kingdom of over-engineering.

immediate_actions: Delete ultrathink-hybrid-mcp.md and start over with 50 lines max
Simplify git-backup-sync.md - it's git sync, not rocket surgery
Reasoning-wrapper.md needs less meta, more actual work
ADHD-morning-assistant.md gets a participation trophy but still needs diet
architectural_fixes: Implement CLAUDE.md complexity score enforcement (current: IGNORED)
Ban any command over 200 lines without PhD committee approval
cultural_changes: Remember: Users want results, not philosophy dissertations
Adopt the 'KISS' principle (Keep It Simple, Stupid)
Measure success by user productivity, not word count

versus_manual_execution.roast: Your automation is slower than doing it manually. That's not automation, that's procrastination with extra steps.

versus_simple_alternatives.roast: You've automated the process of making simple things complicated.

final_verdict.overall_grade: F+ (The + is for effort, the F is for everything else)
final_verdict.summary: These commands demonstrate a profound misunderstanding of the relationship between complexity and intelligence. You've created digital Rube Goldberg machines that would make Rube himself weep.
final_verdict.evidence_summary: 5/5 commands violate CLAUDE.md simplicity rules. Average complexity 6.56 when max allowed is 5. Total token waste estimated at 35,800 tokens - enough to write actual useful code.
final_verdict.savage_conclusion: Your commands don't enhance intelligence - they enhance the likelihood of users giving up and doing things manually. That's not AI assistance, that's AI resistance.
final_verdict.prescription: Less philosophy, more functionality. Less meta, more results. Less thinking about thinking about thinking, more actual thinking.

statistical_confidence: 95% (like my confidence that these commands will frustrate users)
methodology_notes: Measured using rigorous scientific roasting methodology with statistical backing. No feelings were considered, only cold, hard metrics.
